2604.12490.title: Deepfakes at Face Value: Image and Authority
2604.12490.paper_summary: Deepfakes are synthetic media that superimpose or generate someone's likeness on to pre-existing sound, images, or videos using deep learning methods. Existing accounts of the wrongs involved in creating and distributing deepfakes focus on the harms they cause or the non-normative interests they violate. However, these approaches do not explain how deepfakes can be wrongful even when they cause no harm or set back any other non-normative interest. To address this issue, this paper identifies a neglected reason why deepfakes are wrong: they can subvert our legitimate interests in having authority over the permissible uses of our image and the governance of our identity. We argue that deepfakes are wrong when they usurp our authority to determine the provenance of our own agency by exploiting our biometric features as a generative resource. In particular, we have a specific right against the algorithmic conscription of our identity. We refine the scope of this interest by distinguishing between permissible forms of appropriation, such as artistic depiction, from wrongful algorithmic simulation.
2604.12490.paper_summary_zh: 深偽技術是合成媒體，它利用深度學習方法將某人的肖像疊加或生成到已有的聲音、圖像或視頻上。現有對於創建和分發深偽技術所涉及的錯誤的描述，主要集中在它們造成的傷害或違反的非規範性利益上。然而，這些方法並未解釋為何即使深偽技術不造成任何傷害或損害其他非規範性利益，它們仍然可能是錯誤的。為了解決這個問題，本文指出了一個被忽視的原因，為何深偽技術是錯誤的：它們可以顛覆我們對於擁有我們形象的可允許使用權和我們身份治理的合法利益。我們主張，當深偽技術通過利用我們的生物特徵作為生成資源，奪取我們決定自己行為來源的權威時，它們是錯誤的。特別是，我們對於身份的算法徵用擁有特定的權利。我們通過區分可允許的挪用形式（如藝術描繪）與錯誤的算法模擬，來細化這一利益的範疇。
2604.12490.author: James Ravi Kirkpatrick et.al.
